name: Extract content from compound files
description: The process of extracting files and objects contained within compound files, including archives, Office documents, PDFs, and email containers, surfacing the contained items as first-class files for further analysis.
details:
'Compound file' is used here in the broad sense: any file format whose payload is one or more other files. 

This covers user-downloaded archives (.zip, .rar, .7z, .tar), images and objects embedded in Office documents (.docx / .xlsx / .pptx, and legacy .doc / .xls / .ppt that use the Microsoft Compound File Binary Format, attachments and embedded streams in PDFs, and attachments inside email containers. 

Extracting them surfaces each contained item as a first-class file so downstream techniques such as file-type identification, extension-mismatch detection, and hash matching can operate on the content. 

This is distinct from DFT-1045, which decodes archive containers produced by the forensic extraction pipeline (e.g. an iOS file-system extraction packaged as a TAR file); this technique is applied later in the workflow, against compound files encountered within user data. The technique should be applied recursively because extracted items may themselves be compound files.
